data.title: XRP Analyst Flags Biggest Institutional Unlock That The Market Has Ever Seen
data.description:
A popular XRP community figure is making a case that the XRP Ledger is on the cusp of a transformation that would change how institutional capital works with decentralized infrastructure. The comment was based on the newly proposed AMM Swappable Curves standard, which seeks to improve XRPL’s native automated market maker beyond the existing XLS-30 design. The proposal is still at the community review and amendment stage, but it is already a major talking point among XRP supporters.

XRPL’s Native AMM Could Be Set For A Major Amendment

The current XRPL native AMM is based on XLS-30, which brought automated market maker functionality to the XRP Ledger and connected it directly to the network’s decentralized exchange. This allows XRPL trades to tap into AMM pools, the order book, or a mix of both, depending on where liquidity is best available.

The proposed AMM Swappable Curves standard would build on that foundation by introducing a pluggable curve architecture. According to the draft posted under XRPL Standards discussion #547 on GitHub, pool creators would be able to choose the invariant function at pool creation. The initial set includes ConstantProduct, ConcentratedLiquidity, and StableSwap curves, with Smart AMM pools reserved for a later companion specification.

Furthermore, the current XLS-30 model uses a single constant-product structure. Constant-product pools are useful for volatile pairs, but they spread liquidity across the full price range. The new proposal is because this is inefficient for correlated assets, especially stablecoin pairs, FX pairs, and tokenized assets that usually trade close to a narrow value range.

Biggest Institutional Unlock XRP Has Ever Seen

X Finance Bull described the proposed AMM Swappable Curves updates on the XRP Ledger as possibly the biggest institutional unlock XRP has ever seen, and XRPL’s native DEX is about to receive a major liquidity infrastructure upgrade.

According to him, the upgrade is comparable to the kind of innovation that helped turn Uniswap V3 into a dominant DeFi trading venue on Ethereum, but with the XRP Ledger’s advantages of burned fees, fast transaction settlement, and very low transaction cost.

He explained that the main reason institutions may care is execution quality. Large stablecoin swaps between RLUSD and USDC could be carried out with almost zero price impact, which is the kind of standard that banks require before moving serious volume through any venue. From here, tighter FX pair settlement and more practical RWA trading at an institutional scale could follow if liquidity becomes more efficient.

X Finance Bull also pointed to the benefits for capital providers, noting that they could earn stronger returns by focusing liquidity where it matters most instead of spreading it thinly across the entire market. This will create a flywheel effect, where better pools attract more volume, higher volume attracts more liquidity providers, and better liquidity attracts larger institutions. XRPL is becoming competitive with every major DeFi venue on earth.

data.title: Biggest Mover Friday: NetApp Stock Soars 30% as AI Demand Sparks Blowout Guidance
data.description:
NetApp shares surged about 30%on Friday after the data-storage company posted fiscal fourth-quarter results and an outlook that beat expectations.

NetApp reported revenue of $1.948 billion and net income of $404 million for the quarter ended April 24, Reuters reported. The company said demand from hybrid cloud and public cloud customers remained firm, while analysts pointed to 12% revenue growth, 11% public-cloud growth and 13% hybrid-cloud growth.

NetApp also forecast first-quarter revenue of $1.83 billion and said full-year sales should land between $7.3 billion and $7.6 billion, above Wall Street estimates. Investors Business Daily said analysts viewed the guidance as conservative and saw pricing tailwinds that could support revenue.

Barclays raised its target to $199 from $134 and kept Overweight. Wedbush increased its target to $150 from $115 and stayed Neutral, while Morgan Stanley lifted its target to $137 from $88 but kept Underweight, underscoring the split on NetApp's rally.

data.title: Anthropic's $36 Billion AI Chip Deal Draws Apollo, Blackstone
data.description:
Apollo Global Management and Blackstone are working to bring additional investors into a roughly $36 billion debt financing deal that could help Anthropic build the AI infrastructure needed to support its next stage of growth. The financing would be used to purchase Google's custom TPU chips, which Anthropic would then lease, according to people familiar with the matter. Broadcom (AVGO), which helps Google develop those chips, is backstopping payments on the largest portions of the transaction, giving the proposed deal a stronger credit foundation than Anthropic may be able to provide on its own as a high-growth startup.

The deal could mark one of the largest private credit transactions ever and possibly the biggest chip-financing debt transaction to date. For investors, the setup matters because it shows how the AI buildout is moving beyond traditional data center financing and deeper into chip-backed credit structures. Anthropic announced Thursday that it raised $65 billion in a separate funding round that valued the company at $965 billion, marking the first time its valuation surpassed OpenAI's. Shares of Broadcom and Google parent Alphabet (GOOGL) rose in late trading after the news, with Broadcom gaining as much as 1.9% to $434.84 and Alphabet advancing as much as 1.2% to $394.81.

The structure is expected to use a special-purpose vehicle that borrows money, receives an equity investment, buys the TPUs and leases them to Anthropic. Anthropic plans to use the chips at sites in New York, Texas, Louisiana and Indiana, according to one person familiar with the matter. The financing is expected to include roughly $6 billion of A1 notes, $25 billion of A2 notes and $4.5 billion of B notes, though final amounts could change. Broadcom's residual value support agreement would help protect senior A1 and A2 investors if Anthropic fails to make lease payments for a certain period, potentially aligning those notes more closely with Broadcom's investment-grade credit profile while keeping Broadcom and Anthropic at arm's length from the transaction.

data.title: ORIGINCLEAR, INC. 1Q 2026: Revenue $2M, Net income ($2.29M), EPS $0— 10-Q Summary
data.description:
ORIGINCLEAR, INC. reported first-quarter 2026 results with revenue of $2M, up 42.6% from $1.4M a year earlier, and a net loss of ($2.29M) versus a ($656.41K) loss in the prior-year quarter; diluted EPS was $0 for both periods.

Financial Highlights

MetricCurrent quarterPrior year quarterYoY change
Revenue¹$2M$1.4M42.6%
Net income²($2.29M)($656.41K)(248.2%)
Diluted EPS³$0$0—

¹ Reported as “Revenue”. ² Reported as “Net income (loss) attributable to OCLN”. ³ Reported as “Basic and diluted income (loss) per share”.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth was driven by higher equipment contracts and pump station sales, with PWT remaining the primary revenue engine and backlog expected to support 2026 revenue.
  • Company is winding down low-margin MWS operations and shifting from manufacturing toward a water infrastructure financing platform via an Opportunity Zone fund.
  • New joint ventures were announced for mobile wastewater treatment in Texas and for digital infrastructure (including Block40X, Bitmern and Enviromaintment).

Original SEC Filing: ORIGINCLEAR, INC. [ OCLN ] - 10-Q - May. 29, 2026

data.title: Norris Industries, Inc. FY 2026: Revenue $286K, EPS $(0.01) — 10-K Summary
data.description:
Norris Industries, Inc. reported fiscal 2026 revenue of $286K and a net loss of $(661,431), with diluted EPS of $(0.01), as production declines and higher operating costs weighed on results versus the prior year.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue was $286K for FY 2026, down from $329K in FY 2025; YoY change (13.1%).
  • Net income was a loss of $(661,431) for FY 2026, compared with a loss of $(601,076) in FY 2025 (larger loss YoY).
  • Diluted EPS was $(0.01) in FY 2026, unchanged from $(0.01) in FY 2025.

Business Highlights

  • Net proved reserves were approximately 30.27 Mbbl of oil and 66.01 MMcf of gas (41 Mbbl BOE gross); reported increases reflect lower expected production rather than new additions.
  • Oil and gas sales declined to $286K in FY 2026 from $329K in FY 2025, driven by reduced well productivity and maintenance issues.
  • Higher lease operating expenses and general & administrative costs were incurred due to increased well maintenance and the plugging and abandonment of four wells, which reduced production capacity.
  • Management is targeting small Texas acquisitions, re-entries, enhanced oil recovery projects, and joint ventures or buyouts while conserving capital and seeking partners.
  • The company said additional financing will be needed to sustain operations; the majority shareholder may fund selective low‑risk initiatives to help boost production.

Original SEC Filing: Norris Industries, Inc. [ NRIS ] - 10-K - May. 29, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Bridge III SPAC Files for IPO
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Bridge III Acquisition filed for an initial public offering.

The special-purpose acquisition company plans to offer 10 million units for $10 apiece.

Each unit will consist of 1 ordinary share and 1 right, with each right entitling the holder to receive one-eighth of a share upon consummation of an acquisition, according to a securities filing.

Bridge III said it isn't limiting its search for an acquisition target to any industry or geographic location.

The company is led by Chief Executive Yongsheng Liu.

Liu has served as chief executive of multiple other SPACs, including Newbridge, Wealthbridge and Goldenbridge. The latter two combined with Scienjoy and SunCar Technology, respectively, to facilitate their initial public offerings.

Bridge III plans to trade shares on Nasdaq under the ticker BDDD.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: A $38.5 Million California Home Could Break a Price Record — Again — WSJ
data.description:
By Sarah Paynter

When Chad and Cari Peets paid $25 million for their Irvine, Calif., home in 2023, they set a price record for the city. Now, after a $10 million renovation, they are looking to do it again.

The venture capitalist and his wife are listing the six-bedroom, roughly 13,000-square-foot home for $38.5 million.

After purchasing the contemporary-style property, they hired interior designer Erica Bryen to lead the renovation, which was completed this year. The home has slab marble fireplaces, Venetian plaster walls, custom millwork and artisan wall coverings, said Cari, who collaborated with Erica on the design.

There are two kitchens, three offices and a conference room. Chad said he was most excited about the home's wellness suite, outfitted with a gym, steam room, sauna and an outdoor cold plunge.

The house looks out onto a private golf course in Shady Canyon, a gated community with large lots near Newport Beach, said listing agent Rob Giem of Plus Real Estate.

Chad is a managing partner at Newport Beach-based RPT Partners, which invests in tech companies. The Peets have four children, three of them now adults. An interior-design enthusiast, Cari said she has renovated about 25 houses. They initially planned to split their time between Irvine and Newport Beach for most of the year, but recently decided to create a family compound in Bayshores.

Single-family homes in Irvine sold for an average of $2.63 million in the last quarter of 2025, up 3.4% from the same period in 2024, according to real-estate appraisal firm Miller Samuel. Orange County, where the house is located, has limited inventory and high demand, Giem said.

Write to Sarah Paynter at Sarah.paynter@wsj.com

data.title: Canada Technical Recession Chatter Rife But Per-Capita GDP Was Up — Market Talk
data.description: The weakness in Canada's economy in the first quarter marks a second consecutive contraction in GDP, igniting talk of a technical recession. However, National Bank of Canada's Matthieu Arseneau and Alexandra Ducharme says it is important to keep in mind that GDP was held back by an ongoing demographic slowdown. On a per capita basis, GDP posted a decent 0.9% increase. That highlights that these concerns are exaggerated for now, especially as private domestic demand was also up, the economists say. Still, the pair say the 0.1% annualized decline in 1Q GDP was disappointing and 1.6 percentage points lower than the consensus forecast and the Bank of Canada's projection. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

data.title: TJX Companies 1Q 2027: Revenue $14.32B, EPS $1.19— 10-Q Summary
data.description:
TJX Companies reported first-quarter results for fiscal 2027 with net sales of $14.32B and diluted EPS of $1.19, reflecting year‑over‑year revenue and earnings growth versus the prior-year quarter.

Financial Highlights

MetricCurrent quarterPrior year quarterYoY change
Revenue¹$14.32B$13.11B9.2%
Net income²$1.33B$1.04B28.6%
Diluted EPS³$1.19$0.9229.3%

¹ Reported as “Net sales”. ² Reported as “Net income”. ³ Reported as “Diluted earnings per share”.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth was driven by a 6% consolidated comparable‑store sales increase and higher average transaction size.
  • E‑commerce remained steady at about 2–3% of sales while Marmaxx, HomeGoods, Canada and International all posted comparable‑sales gains.
  • Company expansion continued with roughly 3% growth in store count and selling square footage, with net openings across banners and regions.
  • Merchandise margins improved and cost ratios declined; inventories rose about 7% on a per‑store (ex‑transit) basis.
  • Capital investment priorities include store openings and renovations, distribution centers and IT, with fiscal 2027 capex guidance of $2.2–$2.3B.

Original SEC Filing: TJX COMPANIES INC /DE/ [ TJX ] - 10-Q - May. 29, 2026
